chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1425 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders as to maintainability of petition. 22.04.2025 Petitioner, Muhammad Hamid Ansari, is present in person. The petitioner, claiming to be suffering from chronic infirmity consequent to his voluntary use of gutka, has filed this petition against 25 respondents, including administration and private persons, seeking action against officials alleged to be colluding in the proliferation of items injurious to health. The office has raised serious objections as to maintainability inter alia as to how this petition could be entertained in view of Judgment in CP D 2149 of 2017; why recourse to 22A&B CrPC was abjured; no corroboration was available on record to support the omnibus allegations; no order etc. was impugned; and why direct recourse was sought to writ jurisdiction in the presence of alternate efficacious fora of remedy. Petitioner had remained unable to satisfy the court on either count on the last date, 14.04.2025, and same was the case today. Therefore, this petition is found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed in limine along with pending applications. Judge Judge
